intro: Nilgirianthus foliosus is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Acanthaceae and commonly known as the 'Nilgiri Bush'. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nilgirianthus foliosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Nilgirianthus foliosus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed for their health-promoting effects.
